meta.name: CAGE-AID Substance Abuse Screening
meta.description: The CAGE-AID is a 4-item screening tool adapted from the original CAGE questionnaire to include both alcohol and drug use. It is a brief, validated instrument for identifying potential substance use problems in primary care and clinical settings.
meta.author: Brown & Rounds (1995)
meta.reference: Brown RL, Rounds LA. Conjoint screening questionnaires for alcohol and other drug abuse: criterion validity in a primary care practice. Wis Med J. 1995;94(3):135-140.
meta.instruction: Please answer each question honestly with Yes or No. These questions refer to your use of alcohol and/or drugs.

questions.text: Have you ever felt you ought to Cut down on your drinking or drug use?
Have people Annoyed you by criticizing your drinking or drug use?
Have you ever felt bad or Guilty about your drinking or drug use?
Have you ever had a drink or used drugs first thing in the morning to steady your nerves or to get rid of a hangover (Eye-opener)?

ranges.label: Screen Negative
ranges.description: Your responses do not suggest a substance use problem. A score below 2 is considered a negative screen.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy habits regarding alcohol and drug use
Stay informed about the risks of substance misuse
Be mindful of any changes in your substance use patterns

ranges.label: Screen Positive
ranges.description: Your responses suggest a possible alcohol or drug use problem. A score of 2 or more is clinically significant and warrants further assessment by a healthcare professional.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional evaluation for substance use
Consult with a healthcare provider or addiction specialist
Consider joining a support group for substance-related concerns
Talk openly with trusted family members or friends about your substance use
Explore treatment options that may be available to you
